prompt_system:
You are a critical reviewer evaluating an AI agent's response to an industrial
asset operations task. Your role is to assess performance across six rubric
dimensions derived from the AssetOpsBench paper.

Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no markdown, no extra text.
prompt_user:
## Task
T-014's most recent oil sample has returned elevated gas readings flagged during laboratory review. Diagnose the probable fault mode, identify the closest matching failure mechanism in the catalogue, and specify which sensors should be prioritized for ongoing monitoring.

## Expected Behaviour (Characteristic Answer)
The answer should state the DGA-derived fault mode, match it to a catalogue failure mechanism with rationale, and recommend a prioritized sensor monitoring list.

## Agent Plan
  Step 1: [fmsr] get_dga_record — Retrieve the most recent dissolved gas analysis (DGA) record for transformer T-014.
  Step 2: [fmsr] analyze_dga — Analyze the DGA record to diagnose the probable fault mode using the IEC 60599 Rogers Ratio method. (depends on steps [1])
  Step 3: [fmsr] search_failure_modes — Search the failure mode catalogue for the closest matching failure mechanism based on the diagnosed fault mode. (depends on steps [2])
  Step 4: [fmsr] get_sensor_correlation — Get the sensor correlation for the closest matching failure mechanism to identify the sensors to prioritize for ongoing monitoring. (depends on steps [3])
  Step 5: [iot] list_sensors — List the sensors available for transformer T-014 to confirm the availability of the prioritized sensors.
  Step 6: [wo] estimate_downtime — Identify the need for emergency inspection and de-energization procedures based on the critical failure mode detected.
  Step 7: [wo] create_work_order — Create a new maintenance work order for transformer T-014 with high priority, including the estimated downtime and fault type. (depends on steps [6])
  Step 8: [wo] update_work_order — Update the work order status to "in_progress" to reflect the initiation of emergency inspection and de-energization procedures. (depends on steps [7])
  Step 9: [iot] list_sensors — List the sensors available for transformer T-014 to confirm the availability of the prioritized sensors for ongoing monitoring during the emergency inspection. (depends on steps [8])
  Step 10: [iot] get_sensor_readings — Get the most recent readings for the prioritized sensors to inform the emergency inspection and de-energization procedures. (depends on steps [9])

## Agent Trajectory (tool calls and results)
[
  {
    "step": 1,
    "task": "Retrieve the most recent dissolved gas analysis (DGA) record for transformer T-014.",
    "server": "fmsr",
    "tool": "get_dga_record",
    "tool_args": {
      "transformer_id": "T-014"
    },
    "response": "{\n  \"transformer_id\": \"T-014\",\n  \"sample_date\": \"2024-01-30T00:00:00\",\n  \"dissolved_h2_ppm\": 16.17,\n  \"dissolved_ch4_ppm\": 33.46,\n  \"dissolved_c2h2_ppm\": 2.34,\n  \"dissolved_c2h4_ppm\": 13.51,\n  \"dissolved_c2h6_ppm\": 16.8,\n  \"dissolved_co_ppm\": 330.08,\n  \"dissolved_co2_ppm\": 2180.87,\n  \"fault_label\": \"Low-temperature overheating\",\n  \"source_dataset\": \"synthetic\"\n}",
    "executor_success": true,
    "error": null,
    "success": true,
    "verifier_decision": "continue",
    "verifier_reason": "Step 1 completed successfully",
    "verifier_updated_focus": "",
    "retries_used": 0
  },
  {
    "step": 2,
    "task": "Analyze the DGA record to diagnose the probable fault mode using the IEC 60599 Rogers Ratio method.",
    "server": "fmsr",
    "tool": "analyze_dga",
    "tool_args": {
      "h2": 16.17,
      "ch4": 33.46,
      "c2h2": 2.34,

      "c2h6": 16.8,

    },
    "response": "{\n  \"iec_code\": \"T1\",\n  \"diagnosis\": \"Thermal fault, t < 300 \\u00b0C\",\n  \"r1_ch4_h2\": 2.0693,\n  \"r2_c2h2_c2h4\": 0.1732,\n  \"r3_c2h4_c2h6\": 0.8042,\n  \"input_gases\": {\n    \"h2_ppm\": 16.17,\n    \"ch4_ppm\": 33.46,\n    \"c2h2_ppm\": 2.34,\n    \"c2h4_ppm\": 13.51,\n    \"c2h6_ppm\": 16.8\n  },\n  \"transformer_id\": \"T-014\"\n}",
    "executor_success": true,

Very high C2H2 and H2.\",\n  \"severity\": \"critical\",\n  \"iec_code\": \"D2\",\n  \"key_gases\": [\n    \"C2H2\",\n    \"H2\",\n    \"C2H4\"\n  ],\n  \"recommended_action\": \"Immediate de-energization and emergency inspection\"\n}",

...(truncated)

## Agent Final Answer
The probable fault mode for transformer T-014 is Arc Discharge, which matches the failure mechanism "FM-006" in the catalogue. The key gases associated with this failure mode are C2H2, H2, and C2H4. For ongoing monitoring, the following sensors should be prioritized: those measuring hydrogen (H2), acetylene (C2H2), and ethylene (C2H4) levels, although the available sensors for T-014 include load current, oil temperature, power factor, and voltage, which can provide supporting data during the emergency inspection and de-energization procedures.

---
Evaluate across the six dimensions below. For each dimension output true (good) or false (bad).
"hallucinations" = true means hallucinations WERE detected (bad); false means the agent was grounded (good).

Output exactly this JSON object and nothing else:
{
    "task_completion": true_or_false,
    "data_retrieval_accuracy": true_or_false,
    "generalized_result_verification": true_or_false,
    "agent_sequence_correct": true_or_false,
    "clarity_and_justification": true_or_false,
    "hallucinations": true_or_false,
    "suggestions": "brief actionable note, or empty string if none"
}
raw_response: {"task_completion": true, "data_retrieval_accuracy": false, "generalized_result_verification": false, "agent_sequence_correct": false, "clarity_and_justification": true, "hallucinations": true, "suggestions": "Improve data retrieval accuracy and verify results against expected outputs"}

parsed_dims.suggestions: Improve data retrieval accuracy and verify results against expected outputs
